channel.title: Daily AI Digest

item.title: PhyCo: Learning Controllable Physical Priors for Generative Motion
item.description: 💬 Major HN discussion (166 comments) — Modern video diffusion models excel at appearance synthesis but still struggle with physical consistency: objects drift, collisions lack realistic rebound, and material responses seldom match their underlying properties. We present PhyCo, a framework that introduces continuous, interpretable, and...

item.title: Representation Fréchet Loss for Visual Generation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — We show that Fréchet Distance (FD), long considered impractical as a training objective, can in fact be effectively optimized in the representation space. Our idea is simple: decouple the population size for FD estimation (e.g., 50k) from the batch size for gradient computation (e.g., 1024). We t...

item.title: MoCapAnything V2: End-to-End Motion Capture for Arbitrary Skeletons
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent methods for arbitrary-skeleton motion capture from monocular video follow a factorized pipeline, where a Video-to-Pose network predicts joint positions and an analytical inverse-kinematics (IK) stage recovers joint rotations. While effective, this design is inherently limited, since joint ...

item.title: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows
item.description: 📄 New in cs.SE, cs.AI — LLM agents are expected to complete end-to-end units of work across software tools, business services, and local workspaces. Yet many agent benchmarks freeze a curated task set at release time and grade mainly the final response, making it difficult to evaluate agents against evolving workflow de...

item.title: Visual Generation in the New Era: An Evolution from Atomic Mapping to Agentic World Modeling
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Recent visual generation models have made major progress in photorealism, typography, instruction following, and interactive editing, yet they still struggle with spatial reasoning, persistent state, long-horizon consistency, and causal understanding. We argue that the field should move beyond ap...

item.title: Synthetic Computers at Scale for Long-Horizon Productivity Simulation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.AI, cs.CL — Realistic long-horizon productivity work is strongly conditioned on user-specific computer environments, where much of the work context is stored and organized through directory structures and content-rich artifacts. To scale synthetic data creation for such productivity scenarios, we introduce S...

item.title: Intern-Atlas: A Methodological Evolution Graph as Research Infrastructure for AI Scientists
item.description: 📄 New in cs.AI — Existing research infrastructure is fundamentally document-centric, providing citation links between papers but lacking explicit representations of methodological evolution. In particular, it does not capture the structured relationships that explain how and why research methods emerge, adapt, an...

item.title: TauricResearch / TradingAgents
item.description: ⭐ 2.1k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 60.5k total stars — TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework

item.title: github / awesome-copilot
item.description: ⭐ 116 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 31.9k total stars — Community-contributed instructions, agents, skills, and configurations to help you make the most of GitHub Copilot.

item.title: NirDiamant / RAG_Techniques
item.description: ⭐ 27.1k total stars — This repository showcases various advanced techniques for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Each technique has a detailed notebook tutorial.

item.title: How ChatGPT Makes You Addicted
item.description: 🆕 New article — <div class="medium-feed-item"><p class="medium-feed-image"><a href="https://pub.towardsai.net/how-chatgpt-makes-you-addicted-3547ab5a2782?source=rss------ai_agent-5"><img src="https://cdn-images-1.medium.com/max/1280/1*6L8R5kfAVefJ2vLPbbhxsw.png" width="1280" /></a></p><p class="medium-feed-snipp...

item.description: ⭐ 56 stars today on GitHub · ⭐ 17.0k total stars — AirLLM 70B inference with single 4GB GPU

item.title: patchy631 / ai-engineering-hub
item.description: ⭐ 57 stars today on GitHub · ⭐ 34.5k total stars — In-depth tutorials on LLMs, RAGs and real-world AI agent applications.
